974.reason: The error line number should correspond to the line where the last non-terminal before the semicolon is located. Lexer.getLine() returns the current line of the lookahead token (which has skipped whitespace/newlines), causing it to report the line of the next statement or EOF instead of the line where the missing semicolon was expected.

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  The error line number should correspond to the line where the last non-terminal before the semicolon is located. Lexer.getLine() returns the current line of the lookahead token (which has skipped whitespace/newlines), causing it to report the line of the next statement or EOF instead of the line where the missing semicolon was expected.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.14285714285714285 , reason:  Error: Lexer.getLine() returns the line number of the current lookahead token, not the line where the syntax error (missing semicolon) actually occurred. It should report the line of the last successfully parsed token.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.25 , reason:  [Lexer.getLine() returns the current global line counter, which may have advanced past the actual line containing the incomplete statement, leading to wrong error line numbers]

977.reason: Similarly, for missing right parenthesis, the line number should reflect the location of the construct expecting the parenthesis, not the current lookahead position which may have advanced past newlines.

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Similarly, for missing right parenthesis, the line number should reflect the location of the construct expecting the parenthesis, not the current lookahead position which may have advanced past newlines.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.14285714285714285 , reason:  Error: Similar to above, this reports the lookahead line instead of the line containing the construct missing the right parenthesis.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.25 , reason:  [Same issue: line number corresponds to current scan position, not the location of the missing parenthesis]

980.reason: Similarly, for missing right square bracket, the line number should reflect the location of the array access/definition, not the current lookahead position.

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Similarly, for missing right square bracket, the line number should reflect the location of the array access/definition, not the current lookahead position.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.14285714285714285 , reason:  Error: Similar to above, this reports the lookahead line instead of the line containing the construct missing the right square bracket.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.25 , reason:  [Same issue: line number is not tied to the token before the missing bracket]

1440.reason: Error: The Token class lacks a field to store the line number where the token appears, which is necessary for accurate error reporting when the lexer has advanced past the error location.

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.14285714285714285 , reason:  Error: The Token class lacks a field to store the line number where the token appears, which is necessary for accurate error reporting when the lexer has advanced past the error location.

425.reason: [The error reporting uses Lexer.getLine() which returns the current lexer position line number, but by this point the lexer has already advanced past the original statement line due to comment processing or whitespace consumption. The specification requires reporting the line number where the last non-terminal before the semicolon is located, not the current lexer position.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 1.0 , reason:  [The error reporting uses Lexer.getLine() which returns the current lexer position line number, but by this point the lexer has already advanced past the original statement line due to comment processing or whitespace consumption. The specification requires reporting the line number where the last non-terminal before the semicolon is located, not the current lexer position.]

856.reason: [Uses Lexer.getLine() which returns the current scanning line, not the line of the last token in the printf statement, causing incorrect error line number]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.25 , reason:  [Uses Lexer.getLine() which returns the current scanning line, not the line of the last token in the printf statement, causing incorrect error line number]
